generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor controlling the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot"
]

instance GearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox component of the first vertical axis with friction modeling focus"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor providing real-time angular position feedback"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the gearbox providing secondary angular position feedback for friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor input current (I_M) for friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

instance MotorTorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Torque sensor measuring motor output torque (τ_M) from SINUMERIK 840D controller"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorTorqueData
]

instance AngularVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Velocity sensor measuring angular velocity (ω) of the gearbox output"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AngularVelocityData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance MotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorTorqueSensor
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AngularVelocitySensor
]

instance EncoderDifferenceData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionData, GearboxPositionData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance MotorGearboxStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) and Internal Block Diagram (IBD) modeling the first axis composition: permanent magnet synchronous motor, gear transmission, and cycloidal gearbox with rotational (torque τ, angular velocity ω) and current (I_M) flows"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralModelingEnabler
]

instance FrictionModelsLibrary : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink models simulating friction effects (Coulomb-Viscous, Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) for gearbox analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionSimulationEnabler
]

instance ReferenceDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Manufacturer-specific dynamics model generating reference torque values (τ_G, ω) for comparison with measured data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReferenceDataEnabler
]

instance MeasuredDataCollection : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data traces from physical sensors: motor current (I_M), output angle, torque (τ_M), and angular velocity (ω) streamed via MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData, MotorTorqueData, AngularVelocityData
]

instance SimulatedFrictionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Outputs from friction models (τ̂_M, τ̂_G, τ̂_out) derived from MATLAB Simulink simulations for comparison with measured data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EncoderDifferenceData
]

instance SystemTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated torque data (τ_out) combining motor and gearbox outputs for friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorTorqueData
]

instance FrictionAnalysisData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured data traces combining measured (τ_M, ω) and simulated friction models (τ̂_M, τ̂_G) for real-time friction identification"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorTorqueData, AngularVelocityData, SimulatedFrictionData
]

// C11: Enablers
instance StructuralModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) using SysML for structural (BDD, IBD) and behavioral modeling of the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeFrictionIdentification, ControlOptimizationService
]

instance FrictionSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink for simulation of ideal system behavior and friction effects (Coulomb-Viscous, Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre)"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeFrictionIdentification, VisualizationDashboard
]

instance MQTTProtocolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabling real-time data streaming from SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller to digital shadow"
    DTDFVocab:enables MeasuredDataCollection
]

instance AssetAdministrationShellEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell (AAS) for traceability of data sources (motor encoder, gearbox) and their relationships to physical system"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisData
]

instance ReferenceDataEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Manufacturer-specific dynamics models generating reference torque values (τ_G, ω) for system health analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemTorqueData
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeFrictionIdentification : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous, multi-physics-aware identification of joint friction using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre)"
    DTDFVocab:provides StaticFrictionInsight, KineticFrictionInsight, ViscousFrictionInsight
]

instance VisualizationDashboard : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified interface integrating live data, simulated friction models, and reference values (τ_M, τ_G) for semantic visualization of friction loss"
    DTDFVocab:provides DashboardInsight, DataTraceVisualization
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Adjustment of robot control algorithms (via SysML models) to compensate for friction-induced deviations, improving motion accuracy by up to 30%"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlParameterAdjustment, AccuracyImprovementAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance StaticFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of static friction in the gearbox through encoder difference analysis and comparison with simulated Coulomb friction models"
]

instance KineticFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of kinetic friction through analysis of encoder velocity differences and viscous friction components in operational data"
]

instance ViscousFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of viscous friction effects by comparing simulated and measured torque data during angular velocity variations"
]

instance ControlParameterAdjustment : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of robot control parameters (via SysML models) to compensate for friction-induced positional deviations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AccuracyImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual/automatic optimization of control algorithms to achieve improved motion accuracy (±0.06 mm) by reducing friction-induced errors"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualized comparison between measured and simulated friction data (τ̂_M, τ̂_G) showing system health and model accuracy deviations"
]

instance DataTraceVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic visualization linking data points to their computational origins (simulation models in MATLAB Simulink) for traceability"
]

// C1: Environment
instance RobotFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxActuator,
        MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder,
        MotorCurrentSensor, MotorTorqueSensor,
        AngularVelocitySensor,
        MotorGearboxStructuralModel, FrictionModelsLibrary,
        ReferenceDynamicsModel,
        MeasuredDataCollection, SimulatedFrictionData,
        SystemTorqueData, FrictionAnalysisData
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time and dynamic**:
  - **Data acquisition frequency**: Sensor data (I_M, torque τ_M, angular velocity ω) is streamed at **real-time intervals** (not explicitly quantified but implied to be high-frequency, likely sub-second for dynamic friction analysis).
  - **Model updates**: Friction models (white-box/black-box) are continuously refined during operation, with updates triggered by **live data comparisons** between measured and simulated outputs (e.g., motor output torque τ_M, gearwheel output τ_G). The twinning process ensures **low-latency synchronization** between the physical system and the digital shadow for friction detection and control adjustments."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is **purpose-driven and centralized**:
  - **Single digital shadow**: Focuses on the **first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot** (motor + gearbox) without hierarchical internal twins.
  - **Orchestration**: The digital shadow is linked to the **SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller** via MQTT for real-time data exchange. The constellation includes:
    - **Physical system**: The robot’s first axis (motor, gearbox).
    - **Digital shadow**: Real-time representation of friction models and sensor data.
    - **Simulation models**: MATLAB Simulink models for friction analysis.
    - **Dashboard**: Visualization component for insights."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates primarily during the **operational phase** of the physical system:
  - **As operated**: The digital shadow is created and maintained during the robot’s active use in machining tasks to **continuously monitor and refine friction models** for improved motion accuracy. The focus is on **real-time data-driven adjustments** rather than design or manufacturing phases."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity considerations include:
  - **Verification/Validation**:
    - **Sensor calibration**: The two encoders (motor + secondary) reduce positional uncertainty by analyzing encoder differences for elasticity and friction.
    - **Model validation**: Simulation models (MATLAB Simulink) are validated against measured data (I_M, τ_M, ω) to ensure accuracy in friction predictions.
  - **Uncertainty and errors**:
    - **Friction modeling**: White-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models account for wear, load, and temperature variations, but complexity increases with parameter requirements.
    - **Data accuracy**: MQTT streaming ensures low-latency but may introduce minor delays; sensor noise is mitigated via differential encoder analysis.
  - **Uncertainty quantification**: Not explicitly quantified, but implied to be managed via continuous model refinement and sensor redundancy."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The digital twin technical connection involves:
  - **Network protocol**: **MQTT** enables real-time data streaming from the **SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller** to the digital shadow.
  - **Data flow**: One-way data flow from physical sensors (I_M, τ_M, ω) to the digital shadow (passive entity), with processing components (e.g., MATLAB Simulink) generating simulated outputs (ˆτ_M, ˆτ_G, ˆτ_out).
  - **Traceability**: Data traces are linked to their sources (e.g., motor encoder, gearbox) via **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** and SysML models."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to:
  - **Modeling standards**: **SysML** for structural/behavioral modeling (BDD, IBD) and **MATLAB Simulink** for simulation.
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model**: Follows the **Kritzinger et al. [32] / Michael et al. [15]** framework for data traces, models, and processing components.
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Standardized for traceability and interoperability with industrial controllers (e.g., SINUMERIK 840D)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The security and safety considerations include:
  - **Remote operation**: The digital shadow enables **real-time remote monitoring** of the robot’s first axis without direct physical intervention, reducing human exposure to hazards (e.g., machining tools).
  - **Data integrity**: MQTT ensures secure communication between the physical system and the digital shadow, with no explicit mention of encryption or authentication protocols. The focus is on **low-latency data exchange** for friction analysis.
  - **Safety cell**: The robot’s safety cell restricts the working range to [-90°, 90°], mitigating unintended collisions or overshooting."
]
